mechanistic_details.key_intermediate: excited TiATA core transfers electrons to Pd sites

chemistry.scope_summary: Various olefins including styrene, allylbenzene, trans-β-methylstyrene, cyclohexene, and 5-hexen-2-one were successfully hydrogenated with high conversion (>90%) and selectivity (>99%) under visible light irradiation

optimization_table.key_findings: Pd/TiATA@LZU1 shows excellent photocatalytic performance with >99% conversion and >99% selectivity

figures.flow_path: photocatalytic tandem reaction in a dual-chamber microreactor
figures.key_information: Synthesis of Pd doped MOF@COF core-shell hybrid for photocatalytic tandem reaction involving surface adsorption, interfacial growth, Pd2+ incorporation, NaBH4 reduction, and immobilization
figures.caption: Scheme 1.   Schematic scope of the preparation of Pd doped TiATA@LZU1 core–shell and their photocatalytic applications in batch and dual-chamber  microreactor.

Figure 1.   The SEM images of a) TiATA b) LUZ1, and c) the core–shell TiATA@LZU1. d) The  TEM and e) elemental mapping images of TiATA@LZU1. The scale bars in (a)–(e) are 500 nm,  5  µ m, 200 nm, 0.1  µ m, and 250 nm, respectively.
Figure 2.   a) The TEM and b) enlarged images of Pd/TiATA@LZU1 (inset: size distribution of Pd nanoparticles).
Figure 2.   a) The TEM and b) enlarged images of Pd/TiATA@LZU1 (inset: size distribution of Pd nanoparticles).

Figure 3.   Emission spectra of different hybrid catalysts excited at 380 nm  (inset: illustration of the electron transfer process in Pd/TiATA@LZU1).  The PL study was carried out on a suspension with concentration of    100 ppm in MeCN.

Figure 4.   a) Amount of H 2  produced from dehydrogenation of NH 3 BH 3  (AB) under different condition as a function of reaction time. b) Cycling test  of the Pd/TiATA@LZU1 for the dehydrogenation of AB under visible light irradiation.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: longer reaction time required for complete conversion
translation_logic.flow_advantage: significantly reduced residence time with maintained high conversion and selectivity
translation_logic.what_changed: reaction time reduced from 15 min to 1.13 min
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: photocatalyst, solvent, atmosphere, high conversion and selectivity
translation_logic.safety_improvement: better control of reaction conditions in microreactor system

figure_descriptions: Scheme 1 shows preparation and application of Pd/TiATA@LZU1 in batch and dual-chamber microreactor
Multiple characterization figures showing SEM, TEM, and elemental mapping
Photoluminescence studies and dehydrogenation performance data

notes: Well-documented photocatalytic hydrogenation study with clear batch vs flow comparison. Time reduction factor calculated from 15 min batch vs 1.13 min flow residence time. Some quantitative details like exact concentrations and temperatures not specified.
